rulings.Case Title: CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. RAYMOND XIANG ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Cindy Zhang, Dong Qui, Raymond Zhang'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 4, Defendants Cindy Zhang, Dong Qui, Raymond Zhang'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss their positions on (1) the scope of any waiver of the attorney-client privilege resulting from Mr. Zhang's deposition testimony concerning the substance of communications with his attorneys and resulting from the reasons proffered by Mr. Zhang in seeking leave to amend the Second Amended Cross-complaint; and (2) whether and to what extent Mr. Zhang's former counsel waived the work product privilege by seeking leave to amend the Second Amended Complaint. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: OSWALD CASTILLO VS. SMITH GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 12, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT SMITHGROUP INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action and granted as to the second cause of action. As to the first cause of action for overtime, there is a triable issue whether the learned professional exemption applies. As to the second cause of action for waiting time penalties, there is no evidence of willfulness. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN GATE EDITIONS LLC et al VS. PETER MARS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 12, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT PETER MARS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the first cause of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action, and overruled as to the second, third and fifth causes of action. For the first cause of action, although Plaintiffs allege excuse from non-performance at 19, this allegation is neither pled nor incorporated by reference in the first cause of action, and Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to add this allegation. For the fourth cause of action, rescission is not a cause of action, but a remedy, and plaintiffs may seek that remedy under other claims. Plaintiffs have adequately pled facts to allege their abuse of process, fraud and declaratory relief claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pltf'S Supplemental Designation Of Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534), CLYDE COURNALE, CURTIS COURNALE, WILLIAM SCHMETTERER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pltf'S Supplemental Designation Of Expert Witnesses Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to strike Plaintiff's supplemental expert declaration is DENIED. The narrative statement of the general substance of the testimony is sufficient under CCP 2034.260(c)(2). The designation is appropriate under CCP 2034.280 since none of the experts originally designated by Plaintiff are attorneys and plaintiff's supplemental expert is an attorney who is being designated to testify regarding the opinions proffered by Defendant's expert witness attorney. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: LINDA BEAN et al VS. TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; From Pltf Ken Lima
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION Ntc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; From Pltf Ken Lima Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff's objections that requests 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10 purport to require production of documents that are protected by the attorney work product and attorney client privilege is not without merit. However, many non-privileged documents also fall within these categories of documents and should have been produced. Moreover, requests 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, and 17 do not call for privileged documents. Within 30 days of entry of this order Plaintiff shall serve verified code-compliant responses to Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation's first set of requests for production (with the exception of Request Nos. 3, 4, 5, 6, 13, 15 and 17 because TNDC has withdrawn the motion with respect to these requests), and produce responsive non-privileged documents no later than 45 days of entry of this order accompanied by a privilege log Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: LINDA BEAN et al VS. TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; From Pltf Passion Wilson
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION Ntc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; From Pltf Passion Wilson Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff's objections that requests 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10 purport to require production of documents that are protected by the attorney work product and attorney client privilege is not without merit. However, many non-privileged documents also fall within these categories of documents and should have been produced. Moreover, requets 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, and 17 do not call for privileged documents. Within 30 days of entry of this order Plaintiff shall serve verified code-compliant responses to Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation's first set of requests for production, and produce all responsive non-privileged documents no later than 45 days of entry of this order accompanied by a privilege log. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: VERONICA BARBA VS. HEALD COLLEGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Deft Heald College Llc To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 12, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT HEALD COLLEGE, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case. Granted. The arbitration agreement is enforceable, covers the plaintiff's claims and is not unconscionable. Plaintiff has not shown that there is any substantive inconscionability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
